subtitle_ch: 亡国的王储 迈德漠斯
subtitle_en: Amphoreus' Saga of Heroes — Mydeimos, the Fallen Prince

left_ch.text: 在士兵之间，久负盛名的城邦……\n悬锋，\n是每一个战士的向往之地。

right_ch.text: 成为悬锋祭典的冠军，\n是千里挑一…不，万里挑一的殊荣。
「那只是杀戮，毫无荣耀可言。」\n一个男人挡在我面前。
他名叫迈德漠斯……\n万敌。\n是悬锋的王储。

left_en.text: Among the soldiers, there was the famed city-state...\nKremnos,\na place every warrior longed for.

right_en.text: To be crowned champion of the Kremnos Festival\nwas an honor that only the cream of the crop could attain.
"There's no glory to be found in an accolade only obtainable through slaughter."\nA man blocks my way.
His name is...\nMydeimos,\ncrown prince of Kremnos.

left_ch.text: 男人寡言少语，\n但他的事迹广为流传。
是不可思议的故事，\n人们说，
出生就被生父扔下悬崖，\n坠入冥海，漂流九年之久。

right_ch.text: 「这样都能活下来…连死神都抛弃了他啊。」
不死的身躯。\n属于男人的天赐。
就像翁法罗斯的每一位黄金裔…\n是写作「诅咒」的祝福。

left_en.text: Despite being a man of few words,\nnews of his deeds had traveled far and wide.
His tale is an extraordinary one,\nthey say.
Cast off a cliff by his father at birth,\nhe plunged into the Sea of Souls and drifted for nine years.

right_en.text: "Huh, he managed to survive all of that..." "Even the god of death turned him away."
An immortal body.\nA gift bestowed upon the man.
As with every other Chrysos Heir in Amphoreus...\nA blessing, yet also his curse.

left_ch.text: 无言的男人坐在桌边，\n等待我先开口。\n杯中盛满的，是猩红的液体。
听说是悬锋城的传统。\n以鲜血滋养身躯。

right_ch.text: 在广为流传的故事里……\n他们掠走战场上的每一具尸体。
「自以为是的野史学家。」\n被看穿了……\n他的眼神像狮子一样锐利。
「这是石榴汁。」\n男人嗤之以鼻。

left_en.text: The taciturn man is seated at the table,\nexpecting me to break the silence.\nBefore us is a cup, filled to the brim with a crimson liquid.
People considered it a Kremnoan tradition,\nto sustain their bodies with fresh blood.

right_en.text: In popular legends...\nevery body on the battlefield was taken.
"Arrogant amateur historian."\nHe sees straight through my pretense...\nHis gaze as keen and piercing a lion's.
"This is pomegranate juice."\nThe man scoffs.

left_ch.text: 野史之所以为野史，\n是因为悬锋城消失已久。
人们的神陷入癫狂，\n城邦也迷失在雾中。

right_ch.text: 传闻，悬锋城的末日之战，\n男人也投身沙场。
横扫千军，浴血鏖战，\n用长矛贯穿了敌王——
只是那矛头……\n指向自己的故乡。

left_en.text: The reason it's called a wild tale,\nis because Castrum Kremnos has long vanished.
When their god descended into madness,\nthe city likewise became shrouded in fog.

right_en.text: Rumor has it that during the final battle of Castrum Kremnos,\nthis man, too, took to the battlefield.
Cutting down countless foes, fighting in a sea of blood,\nhe drove his spear through the heart of the enemy king—
Yet the tip of that spear...\nwas directed at his own homeland.

left_ch.text: 议论声起，\n男人起身离去。
流言的真相，\n他从来只用背影讲述。
那是经受千难百死，\n却不曾留下一道伤痕的脊背。

right_ch.text: 迈德漠斯，往日的英雄……\n他是悬锋的叛徒，\n弑父的王储。

left_en.text: A chatter breaks out among the people,\nand the man rises to leave.
The truth of such rumors,\nhe speaks only through his distant silhouette.
A silhouette of a body that has withstood endless trials and faced death innumerable times,\nbut bore not a single mark.

right_en.text: Mydeimos, a hero of yore..\nNow a traitor to Kremnos,\nthe patricidal crown prince.

chapters.title_en: Nations of the World, Why Do You Flee?

left_ch.text: 从何时起，\n他以「万敌」之名为人所知？

right_ch.text: 这英勇力敌万邦，\n而男人不以为意——
唯王权可终结王权，\n唯纷争可终结纷争，
征战的铁蹄踏遍荒野，\n却终归要沾上故乡的血。

left_en.text: Since which day,\nDid he become known as "Mydei"?

right_en.text: His strength rivals all lands,\nBut the man pays it no heed —
For only the authority of a king can end another king,\nAnd only strife can end strife.
The torrent of conquest crosses the barrens,\nYet with homeland's blood, it is destined to be stained.

left_ch.text: 无人敢与他握手致意。
那双手炙如烈阳，\n浸染着火的伟力。

right_ch.text:
那是何等壮举——
折戟的沙场，
男人为逝去的士兵掘墓，
徒手将千军埋葬。
那一战……\n他捏碎了所有来敌的兵刃。
不死的身躯万折不摧，\n却难奈苦泪在心间流淌。

left_en.text: None dare to shake his hand in greeting.
For his hands burn as the blazing sun,\nSoaked in the might of the flames.

right_en.text:
What a mighty feat it is —
In the aftermath of defeat,
The man digs graves for the deceased soldiers,
Burying thousands with his own hands.
In that fight...\nHe crushed all invading blades with his fists.
A body immortal may never fall,\nBut tears of pain in his heart still flow.

left_ch.text: 「数年的漂流，使他厌恶水波。」\n传言，那是男人不愿流泪的理由。
可在哀地里亚的风雪中，\n男人也曾落下过一滴，\n绽作染血的百合。

right_ch.text: 如其不死神躯，\n花朵永不凋谢，

假以时日，万物皆成灰烬……
此世最后的美景，
将由这孤高的男人留下，
尽管无人相信。

left_en.text: "His years of drifting made him detest water's waves."\nThey say, that is the reason he's so unwilling to weep.
But in Aidonia's blizzards he once did cry,\nA tear shed among the storm and frost,\nThat bloomed into a lily stained with blood.

right_en.text: Like his undying body,\nThe flower never wilts.

In time, the entire world will turn to ash...
This haughty man shall be,
The one to leave behind the world's final beauty.
Though none are willing to believe.

left_ch.text:
不同于族人，
那纹饰是以男人的鲜血刻写。
此事疑点重重，
令凡庸的野心躁动。
「伤及万敌的锋刃，必定足以弑神。」\n人们至今仍在找寻。\n然而那也只是他遵循的传统……

right_ch.text: 不可使敌人发觉你的血液滴落，\n以此使人深信，\n眼前之人无可匹敌。

left_en.text:
Unlike his kin,
Those patterns are carved with his blood.
A matter so suspicious,
It stirs the ambitions of the mundane.
"A blade that can cut through the mighty must be enough to slay gods."\nThe people to this day still seek it,\nYet it is simply a tradition he follows closely...

right_en.text: Do not let your enemy notice that you're bleeding,\nAnd thus convince them,\nThat you are someone unsurpassed, a warrior supreme.

left_ch.text: 前尘旧事，铸就他的身躯。\n「除了悬锋的传统，你还剩下什么？」\n白发的友人曾向他提问。

left_en.text: The stories of the past forged his body.\n"Apart from Kremnos' traditions, what do you have left?"\nThe white-haired friend once asked.

left_ch.text: 男人沉默不语，\n只是瞪视天际，找寻迢遥的过去。
那流离的岁月，\n同一个问题，被千百人问起。\n而他从不否认——

right_ch.text: 「正因如此，我才踏上此程。」

left_en.text: The man goes silent and still,\nHis gaze burning into the horizon, looking for a distant past.
In his days of wandering, this same question,\nWas asked by thousands upon thousands,\nAnd he never denied —

right_en.text: "That is why I walk on this path."

chapters.title_en: The Day the Fierce Lion Departed

left_ch.text: 那战场无人能近，\n悬锋的孤军，横于逐火的道路，

right_ch.text: 他命令自己的军队袖手旁观——\n那是王储与英雄，最初的会晤。
两个男人死战十夜，\n直至角斗迎来终局，
他们未能从彼此手中取得一胜，\n也从未留下一负。

left_en.text: None may approach that battlefield.\nThe lone army of Kremnos, an obstacle on the Flame-Chase Journey.

right_en.text: He ordered his troops to stand aside —\nThat was the first meeting between hero and prince.
The two men fought for ten nights unceasingly,\nTill the combat drew to its end.
Neither was able to score a single victory,\nAnd conceded not a single defeat.

left_ch.text: 无人知晓，\n为何男人的命运，总是被某个数字笼罩。
正如无人确信，\n逐火的英雄们，\n是如何将男人说服。

right_ch.text: 惺惺相惜的说法并不尽然。\n记忆当中，\n母亲只对他说过七句言语。
阴差阳错，那纺织金线的领袖——\n复述了其中之一。

left_en.text: None know why this man's fate,\nIs always overshadowed by a single number decreed.
Just like how none can ascertain,\nHow the Flame-Chasing heroes\nConvinced this man.

right_en.text: It was not mutual appreciation. Nay.\nFor in his memories,\nSeven sentences were all his mother to him ever spake.
Yet somehow, that leader who weaves the golden threads — \nRecounted one of the seven with ease.

left_ch.text: 男人心中无谓对错，\n「狮子拥有自己的史官。」
「狩猎的历史也不应为猎人独享。」\n因此，他寡言少语。

right_ch.text: 曾有人这样猜测，\n他将圣城视作家园，\n是为给自己寻觅归处。

身为王者，
那也是他赐予子民的第一样事物。
可万物皆是如此——
一旦送出，自己便无法拥有。

left_en.text: There is no right or wrong in the man's heart.\n"The lion has its own historian."
"And the history of the hunt should not be held by the hunter alone."\nAnd thus, he speaks little.

right_en.text: Others once guessed,\nHe regards the holy city as his home,\nFor he seeks a land to belong.

As the king to his people,
It is the first gift to them he brings.
But as per the rule with gifts —
Once given away, the gifter will never re-possess this thing.

left_ch.text: 旅途漫漫，\n从无一人敢触怒男人。
并非由于他那桀骜心性——\n而是英雄们的每一餐，\n均由他亲手烹煮，技艺超凡。

right_ch.text: 目视在山林中并不可信，\n自少年起，\n他便通过气味分辨来者，因而得益。
其中亦有例外——\n男人的宿敌常常疑惑，\n为何自己的每一餐都难以下咽。

left_en.text: The journey is long,\nYet none ever dares to anger the man.
Not because of his unrestrained manners —\nBut because every meal the heroes partook,\nWere all cooked by his peerless skill.

right_en.text: One's sight serves falsehoods in the woods.\nHe has relied on scent since young\nTo distinguish guests and obtain gains.
Yet there are always exceptions —\nThe man's archenemy often puzzled,\nWhy their food always borderline nauseating.

left_ch.text: 踏足圣城之日难以忘却——\n信仰纷争之人，\n必将身受非议。
「虫蛾都会争相吞吃，那男人是疯王的血裔。」

当然无妨，
男人本就以大敌自居。
肃清前路，是为让同伴有朝一日，
配得上自己的怒意。

right_ch.text: 可直至预言成真……\n圣城从未有过一人，\n被他伤害过一毫一分。

left_en.text: He still recalls the day he entered the holy city —\nOne who worships strife,\nIs bound to have rumors rife.
"Even insects consume each other, and he's no less than the Mad King's kin."

It matters little,
For the man always considered himself an enemy to the world.
He paves the road so that one day his peers,
Would match his anger within.

right_en.text: Yet, until the prophecy turned true...\nNone in the holy city,\nHe ever did ruin.

chapters.title_en: The Lone King Slays God, Becomes God

left_ch.text: 「为荣誉而死并非永眠——」 这是悬锋城人尽皆知的谎言， 「死后的世界沐浴晨光，远胜凡尘。」 然而，王储不会轻信玄虚的诗篇。

right_ch.text: 「不敢正视死亡的懦夫们…」 自冥流归来的那日，他对众人宣告， 「念诵自欺的妄语，盼望牺牲的仙境…」 那话语充斥不敬， 比他的血统还要高傲—— 「真正的勇士，应对那福地嗤之以鼻。」

left_en.text: "To die for glory is not eternal sleep —" This is a lie all Castrum Kremnos keeps. "The world after death is superior to reality and basks in light." But such vague poems the crown prince would not easily believe.

right_en.text: "Cowards who dare not to look death in the eye..." He declared thus on the day he returned from the stream of souls. "Longing for a martyrs' heaven and reciting self-deceiving stories..." His words are sacrilege reincarnate, More arrogant than his blood and family — "True warriors should treat that paradise with spite."

left_ch.text:
诸多从者因此言却步，
不再随他前行。

只因他们感到这少年的言语，
比诗篇还要荒诞不经。

left_en.text:
Many followers thus stop,
No longer following his steps.

For they feel this young man's words,
Compared to poems, are even more absurd.

left_ch.text: 不死之躯， 从不等同「全无缺失」， 为寻命门， 男人数次只身涉险。

right_ch.text:
彼时他仍青涩，
技艺尚需苦练，
于求死不得的厄境中，
知晓天赐因何亦名为诅咒——

他在生命的边界，
窥见了那可怖之相。

left_en.text: A body immortal, Equals not "perfection." The man braved dangers endless, Simply to seek his weakness.

right_en.text:
In his greener days
With more amateur skills,
In crises where death denied him even after kills,
He learned why "curse" is a synonym for "blessing,"

For he saw that visage of terror
at life's closing.

left_ch.text:
提及此事时，
男人全无波澜。
来去绝境足足九回，
曾被视为缺失的，
如今他都已强胜众人百倍。

right_ch.text: 不过，\n问及他是否仍如幼时那般怕痒，\n他始终避而不答。

left_en.text:
The man seems unmoved
when this past is mentioned.
Having braved death nine times,
He who was once lacking,
Now excels above everyone else, still rising.

right_en.text: Yet,\nWhen asked if he's still as ticklish as when he was a child,\nHe does not answer.

left_ch.text:
那是他必然不屑一顾的野史——
据说悬锋城拥有神赐的秘仪，
王的力量以此代代相传…
这是他们独挡千军的奥秘。

left_en.text:
It's a wild tale he won't care to opine —
It's said that Castrum Kremnos has secrets divine,
Through which the king's power has passed,
A one-man army, by strength in their bloodline.

left_ch.text: 「因而，未能继位的叛徒，\n是最为羸弱的王者。」

right_ch.text: 此言传入故事的主角耳中， 他并未驳斥，坦然而对： 「这并非传言。」 「但若来日功盖列王，往事又有何妨？」

left_en.text: "Therefore, a traitor who does not inherit the throne,\nIs a king most infirm."

right_en.text: As the protagonist hears these words, He refutes not, but openly responds: "These are not rumors unfounded." "But will they matter if my deeds eclipse those of all other kings, achieving glory beyond?"

left_ch.text: 「战不旋踵，他侵夺着我们的荣誉。」\n这句军中的碎语，\n时常落在男人不摧的心端。
举凡亲征的战役陷入绝境，\n他都将军队遣往安稳的阵地，\n孤身一人，以死继战。

right_ch.text:
追随他的悬锋孤军们，
大都寸功未立，寿终正寝，
他们临终之际时有怨言，
可男人却毫无愧意——
「你的荣耀不在死亡，而是在我掌心。」

left_en.text: "Never turning back in war, he seizes all our glory."\nThat rumor in the war camp of fury,\nOften casts a shadow on the man's heart, indestructible and fiery.
When campaigns become mired in terror,\nHe sends his army to places safe and secure,\nYet alone he goes to fight, knowing death can touch him ne'er.

right_en.text:
The Kremnoan Detachment who follow him in livery,
All die in bed, devoid of glory.
They have their share of grudges upon death,
But the man has no regret, none necessary.
"Your glory lies not in death but in my hands."

left_ch.text:
尘埃落定，
纷争已息。
故乡唯余死寂，
男人孤身回返。

right_ch.text: 往日的幻影似在发问——\n「你已走完那逐火之路了吗，王？」

left_en.text:
The dust settles,
And Strife calms.
A deathly quiet covers his father's realm,
And the man alone returns home.

right_en.text: A question seems to sound, from shadows of the past around —\n"Did you already walk that Flame-Chasing Road, O King?"

left_ch.text: 于王座之上， 男人沉吟—— 「没有什么值得再去逐猎，」 「朕已是旅途尽头的烈火。」

right_ch.text:
如其所言，
弑王成王，弑神登神。

浴血代冠，是他永恒的宿命。

left_en.text: Atop the throne, The man mutters alone — "There's nothing else worthy of the hunt," "For I am the blazing fire at the journey's end."

right_en.text:
As it has been foretold,
He killed the king to become a king, and killed the god to become a god.

His eternal destiny — is to be crowned in blood.

chapters.title_en: A Glory That Will Never Die

left_ch.text: 一切如前——\n若想窥探男人的怒火，\n须在七千步外远望。

right_ch.text: 由他据守的悬锋， 再一次被雾霭笼罩， 铁锈、鲜血与火， 是歌颂半神的典仪。
今时无人见证，此刻无人欢呼，\n最后的悬锋祭典，永无停息。

left_en.text: As always —\nTo perceive the man's fury,\nOne must do so seven thousand paces away.

right_en.text: The Kremnos he guards Is once again shrouded by mist. Rust, blood, and fire Are the ceremonial ode to the demigod.
There is no one to witness or to give cheers of glory,\nThe final Kremnos Festival goes on without end.

left_ch.text: 冠军拥获死寂，\n而他的荣光，\n皆由败军铭记。

left_en.text: The victor shall embrace death and silence,\nAnd his glory\nWill only be remembered by the defeated array.

left_ch.text: 于男人而言，\n法度刻印已无意义。
鏖战片刻不曾停歇，\n日复一日，难分今晨昨夜，

遗骸逐层堆积，
垒作深渊，砌成山巅。
他以这尖峰划分时光，
用战火丈量流年。

right_ch.text: 偶尔，\n狮子会攀上绝顶，\n俯瞰尘世，寻觅故人。
那遥远的天地开阔无边，\n而开阔的天地，也愈发遥远。

left_en.text: For that man,\nLaws and seals no longer have meaning.
War and battle never cease for a single minute.\nEach passing day indistinguishable, morning and night entwined.

The corpses pile up,
Filling ravines, forming mountains strong.
He carved time with peaks' edge,
And counted the fleeting years by fires of war.

right_en.text: Sometimes,\nThe lion would climb to the hilltop\nTo look down on the world and search for friends in longing.
The distant heavens and earth stretched vast and boundless,\nBut their endlessness felt ever more remote.

left_ch.text: 如尼卡多利那般，\n终有一日，他也难逃疯狂。

right_ch.text: 独守孤城，恶战连连，\n黑潮的呢喃，常在他耳边回荡。
可每一瞬间，焰火自胸膛燃起，\n将那不屈的来路照亮。

left_en.text: Like Nikador,\nHe cannot escape madness's clutches.

right_en.text: Guarding the city alone and embroiled in war,\nThe murmurs of the black tide constantly sound in his ears.
Yet a flame soars up his chest every moment,\nCasting light upon the indomitable path behind him.

left_ch.text:
追忆为往事作结，
男人再度迈入战阵，
他已是这世间必要的伤痛，
是令旧神称臣的新王。

left_en.text:
Memories seal the echoes of the past,
As the man strides into battle, fists clenched fast.
He is the wound this world must bear,
The new king before whom old gods despair.

left_ch.text: 如哀地里亚的诗歌所言，

人们闭目降生，
是为适应世间光明。
而老去时视力衰微，
是为再度熟悉黑暗。

right_ch.text: 然而男人不以衰老，\n拥抱死亡的召唤。

每一次逝而归返的，
他都怒目圆睁，瞪视来敌，
直至一切猝然褪去的，
如同黑潮翻涌，万物消散。

left_en.text: As stated in Aidonia's poems,

Humans are born with eyes closed
To accustom to this world's light,
And their sight fades in old age
To reacquaint with night's anthems.

right_en.text: But the man did not bow to the decay of time,\nRejecting the lure of death's call.

With each return from the brink of death,
His eyes burn with fury, fixed upon his foes,
Until everything suddenly faded away,
Like a surging black tide swallowing all into nothingness.

left_ch.text: 黑潮翻涌，万物消散。
狮子终有一死，\n此去再无复还。

轰然陨落的，
是被贯穿的胸膛，
静静离去的，
是金血淌入冥湍。

right_ch.text: 「……」

left_en.text: The world dissipates in the roiling black tide's requiem.
Even the lion will one day die,\nA departure with no way back.

With a sudden fall,
His pierced chest hits the ground
And slowly flowing into the underworld
Is his golden blood, departing his system.

right_en.text: "..."

left_ch.text:
可那目光如炬，
至死不熄，
贯穿盗火的身影，
直达迢遥的天际。

right_ch.text: 以至明日，星星会如此传唱： 悬锋的孤王，其人名为万敌。 他一生行向的，是永劫战火， 他身后留下的，是荣光遍地。

left_en.text:
But that gaze, fierce as flame,
Never dimmed, not even in death,
Looking beyond those flame-seeking figures
Reaching far toward the endless horizon.

right_en.text: In the morrow's sky, the stars will chant: The lone king of Kremnos is called Mydei. His journey was one of ceaseless, eternal warfare, And behind him, he left a world ablaze with glory.
